name: Sant Pere de les Maleses-Collegats Trail
name_original: Running - Ruta circular de la Cueva de la Serpiente
description: Jog this moderate 4.6-mile (7.4 km) trail through Collegats Gorge, featuring the Sant Pere de les Maleses Monastery and Cova de la Serpent.
region_intro: Jogging routes around La Pobla de Segur offer diverse terrain in the Pallars Jussà region of the Catalan Pyrenees. The area features a wide altitudinal range, from agricultural plains to dense forests and steep cliffs. Key natural features include the distinctive red cliffs of Rocs de Queralt, the large Sant Antoni Reservoir, and the Noguera Pallaresa River. This varied landscape provides a range of running experiences. Best jogging routes around La Pobla De Segur The most popular jogging route is Sant Pere de les Maleses-Collegats Trail, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) moderate trail that takes about 1 hour to complete, offering views of the mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from la Pobla de Segur, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.0 km) path that follows riverside sections and offers views of the town. Local runners also love the Rutilla loop from la Pobla de Segur, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around La Pobla de Segur is defined by mountainous terrain, the Sant Antoni Reservoir, and the Noguera Pallaresa River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation changes. The routes in La Pobla de Segur are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore La Pobla de Segur's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in La Pobla de Segur?
A: La Pobla de Segur offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 20 trails listed on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy riverside paths to more challenging mountain trails.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in La Pobla de Segur?
A: Yes, La Pobla de Segur has options for beginners. While many routes are moderate to difficult, you can find easier paths, especially along the Noguera Pallaresa River and around the Sant Antoni Reservoir. These areas provide scenic and relatively flat options for a gentle jog.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around La Pobla de Segur?
A: The terrain around La Pobla de Segur is incredibly diverse. You'll find everything from agricultural plains at lower elevations to dense forests, steep cliffs, and high mountain peaks. Many routes feature riverside sections, paths around the large Sant Antoni Reservoir, and trails winding up the distinctive red cliffs of Rocs de Queralt.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in La Pobla de Segur?
A: For families, the easily accessible paths along the Noguera Pallaresa River and around the Sant Antoni Reservoir are excellent choices. These routes are generally less strenuous and offer pleasant scenery, making them suitable for all skill levels and a great way to enjoy the outdoors together.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in La Pobla de Segur?
A: While specific dog rules for each trail are not detailed, the region's extensive network of outdoor trails generally welcomes dogs, especially in natural areas. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: Which running routes offer the best panoramic views in La Pobla de Segur?
A: Many trails in the region provide spectacular panoramic views. Routes that ascend towards viewpoints like Santa Magdalena and Sant Miquel offer rewarding vistas of the Sant Antoni Reservoir and the town. Additionally, paths along the Rocs de Queralt (red cliffs) provide unique backdrops and excellent views of the surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in La Pobla de Segur?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.0 km path that follows riverside sections. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rutilla loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 9.0 km through varied terrain.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see along the running routes?
A: As you run, you can encounter several notable features. The distinctive red cliffs of Rocs de Queralt offer a unique geological backdrop. The vast Sant Antoni Reservoir is a central feature, and you might pass by highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortés&lt;/a&gt; or even the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2698547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argenteria Cliffs and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5164268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salàs de Pallars Historic Village&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby for a cultural stop.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in La Pobla de Segur?
A: La Pobla de Segur is a hub for outdoor activities, and many trailheads, especially those starting directly from the town or popular recreational areas, typically have parking facilities. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for precise parking information for your chosen trail.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in La Pobla de Segur?
A: The diverse altitudinal range of the Pallars Jussà region means running is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails. During summer, riverside paths and routes around the reservoir provide opportunities for cooling off. Winter running is possible, though higher elevations may experience snow.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in La Pobla de Segur?
A: The running routes in La Pobla de Segur are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the continuous change of scenery, from agricultural plains to forests and cliffs, and the stunning views of the Sant Antoni Reservoir and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. La Pobla de Segur offers 11 difficult routes for experienced runners seeking a challenge. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rutilla loop from la Pobla de Segur&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24554926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Font de l'Ús loop from Sossís&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation changes and varied terrain, providing a demanding workout amidst breathtaking landscapes.
tips: Monastery that is located at the entrance of the Collegats gorge, in the municipality of Pobla de Segur. Romanesque style and built with the help of the same rock of the mountain. Legend has it that the bishops robbed and killed travelers who stayed at the monastery.
Cave that we found right next to the path. As a curiosity, legend has it that this cave was inhabited by a snake that ate any animal or traveler that passed by.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sant Pere de les Maleses-Collegats Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied experience, traversing through pine forests and along paths at the base of rocky outcrops. You can expect both ascents and descents, with some sections being quite steep and potentially stony. Other parts feature a smoother up and down, and you'll also pass through holm oak and oak woods.

faqs.question: Where is the best place to start this jogging route?
faqs.answer:
The

Figuereta Fountain

is a common and convenient starting and ending point for the circular excursion along this trail.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I see along the way?
faqs.answer:
You'll encounter several impressive features, including the ruins of the ancient Monastery of Sant Pere de les Maleses, which is built into a large rock shelter. The route also takes you through the dramatic

Collegats Gorge

with its towering rock walls, and past the intriguing Cova de la Serpent. You'll also enjoy panoramic views of the

Noguera Pallaresa river

and the

Rocs de Queralt

.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: The specific jogging route described is rated as 'moderate', requiring good fitness. While some sections are paved and suitable for various skill levels, other running options in the area can be 'hard', demanding very good fitness and sure-footedness. The main circular route to the monastery is generally considered to have no particular difficulty for those with moderate fitness.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, so an early start is advisable, while winter might bring colder conditions, though the area typically remains accessible.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Sant Pere de les Maleses-Collegats Trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on the trail. However, as it's a protected natural area, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially birds of prey, and to respect other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for the Collegats Gorge area?
faqs.answer: The Collegats Gorge is a protected natural area. While there isn't typically an entrance fee for accessing the general trail network, it's always wise to check local regulations for any specific permits required for certain activities or areas, especially if you plan to deviate from marked paths. You can find more information on local tourism websites like spain.info for the latest updates.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area is known for its opportunities to observe various species of birds of prey, soaring above the impressive rock formations of the gorge. Keep an eye out for them as you jog along the route.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer:
Yes, this trail is part of a larger network. It fully encompasses the

Congost de Collegats

and

Sant Pere de les Maleses-Collegats

trails. It also shares significant sections with the

Ramat de Camins. Etapa1 : Font de la Figuereta => Baro

trail, covering about 86% of it, and briefly intersects with various

Via ferrada

routes.

faqs.question: Is there parking available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer:
Yes, parking is typically available near common starting points like the

Figuereta Fountain

. It's advisable to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot, as this is a popular area.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, sturdy jogging shoes with good grip are essential. Layers of clothing are recommended to adapt to changing temperatures, especially if you start early or jog later in the day. Don't forget water, snacks, sun protection, and a map or GPS device, as some sections can be remote.

waypoints.name: Monastery of Sant Pere de les Maleses
